info.description: This is the API documentation for the backend service
info.title: Backend API

get.description: Returns all cleanup policies

post.description: Creates a new cleanup policy and reloads the scheduler

put.description: Updates an existing cleanup policy and reloads the scheduler

delete.description: Deletes a cleanup policy and reloads the scheduler

post.description: Executes a cleanup policy immediately. Use ?dry_run=true to preview matches without acting.

get.description: Returns all notification channels with subscription counts

post.description: Creates a new notification channel

get.description: Returns all known event types that channels can subscribe to
Returns a notification channel by ID

delete.description: Deletes a notification channel and its subscriptions

get.description: Returns paginated delivery logs for a notification channel
get.summary: List delivery logs for a channel

get.description: Returns event type subscriptions for a channel

put.description: Replaces all event type subscriptions for a channel

post.description: Sends a test payload to the channel's webhook URL

get.description: Lists all Kubernetes namespaces matching the stack-* pattern that have no corresponding stack instance in the database. Pass ?details=true to include resource counts and helm releases per namespace (expensive).

parameters.description: Include resource counts and helm releases (true/false)
parameters.name: details

delete.description: Verifies the namespace is orphaned, uninstalls all Helm releases, and deletes the Kubernetes namespace

get.description: Returns high-level aggregate counts (templates, definitions, instances, deploys, users)
Returns usage analytics for each template including definition count, instance count, deploy counts, and success rate
Returns usage analytics per user including instance count, deploy count, and last active time (admin only)
List audit logs with optional filters and pagination. Supports cursor-based pagination for efficient large dataset traversal.

parameters.description: Cursor from previous page for cursor-based pagination (overrides offset)
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Export audit logs as CSV or JSON file download

parameters.description: Export format: csv or json (default: json)
parameters.name: format

post.description: Authenticate with username and password, returns a JWT access token and sets a refresh token cookie
Revokes the current refresh token and blocklists the access token
Revokes all refresh tokens for the current user

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's information
Generates PKCE parameters and state, returns the IdP authorization URL for the frontend to redirect to

parameters.description: Frontend URL to return to after authentication
parameters.name: redirect

get.description: Handles the IdP callback after user authentication. Exchanges the authorization code for tokens, provisions or updates the local user, and redirects to the frontend with a JWT.

post.description: Generates a session ID and OIDC authorization URL for CLI-based SSO login. The CLI opens the returned login_url in a browser and polls cli-token until authentication completes. Optionally accepts a loopback `redirect_uri` (http scheme, any loopback IP such as 127.0.0.1 or [::1], or hostname "localhost", with an explicit port) for the RFC 8252 native-app flow — the callback then 302-redirects the browser directly to that URL with tokens in the query string, no polling needed.
Returns the current status of a CLI auth session. Returns pending while waiting for the user to complete browser authentication, or completed with a JWT token once done.

get.description: Returns public OIDC configuration for the frontend (enabled status, provider name, local auth availability)

post.description: Issues a new access token using the refresh token cookie. Rotates the refresh token (old one invalidated, new one issued).

401.description: Invalid, expired, or revoked refresh token

post.description: Create a new user account (admin only, or when self-registration is enabled)

get.description: Returns all registered clusters. Kubeconfig data is never included in responses. Admin/DevOps users receive full cluster details; other roles receive a summary (id, name, is_default only).

post.description: Registers a new Kubernetes cluster with the provided kubeconfig data.

get.description: Returns a single cluster by ID. Kubeconfig data is never included. Admin/DevOps users receive full cluster details; other roles receive a summary (id, name, is_default only).

put.description: Updates cluster metadata and/or kubeconfig. If kubeconfig is updated, the cached client is invalidated.

delete.description: Removes a cluster registration. Blocked if any stack instances reference this cluster.

post.description: Sets the specified cluster as the default cluster for deployments.
post.summary: Set a cluster as the default

get.description: Returns per-node health, conditions, and capacity for a cluster.
Returns node count, CPU/memory totals, and namespace count for a cluster.
Returns all stack-* namespaces in the cluster.
Returns the resource quota configuration for a cluster, or 404 if not set.
get.summary: Get resource quota config for a cluster

put.description: Creates or updates the resource quota configuration for a cluster. Admin only.
put.summary: Create or update resource quota config for a cluster

delete.description: Removes the resource quota configuration for a cluster. Admin only.
delete.summary: Delete resource quota config for a cluster

get.description: Returns all shared values for the specified cluster, sorted by priority (lowest first).
get.summary: List shared values for a cluster

post.description: Creates a new shared values entry for the specified cluster.
post.summary: Create shared values for a cluster

put.description: Updates an existing shared values entry for the specified cluster.

delete.description: Deletes a shared values entry from the specified cluster.

post.description: Tests connectivity to a cluster by attempting to reach the Kubernetes API server.

get.description: Returns per-namespace resource usage for all stack namespaces in the cluster.
Returns aggregated dashboard data: cluster health, recent deployments, expiring instances, and failing instances.
Returns all favorited entities for the current user
get.summary: List favorites for the authenticated user

post.description: Add an entity to the user's favorites (idempotent)

get.description: Check whether the authenticated user has favorited a specific entity
get.summary: Check if an entity is favorited

delete.description: Remove an entity from the user's favorites

get.description: List branches for a given repository URL
Get the status of all configured Git providers
Check if a branch exists in the given repository
Get a list of all items

post.description: Create a new item with the provided information

get.description: Get an item by its ID

delete.description: Delete an item by its ID

get.description: List the authenticated user's notifications with optional filters and pagination
Returns the unread notification count for badge display
Get the authenticated user's notification preferences

put.description: Update the authenticated user's notification preferences (array of event_type + enabled)

post.description: Mark all of the authenticated user's notifications as read
Mark a single notification as read (verifies ownership)

get.description: Ping test endpoint
List stack definitions with server-side pagination

200.description: Paginated list with data, total, page, pageSize

post.description: Create a new stack definition
Import a stack definition from a portable JSON bundle, creating a new definition with fresh IDs

get.description: Get a stack definition by ID, including its chart configurations

delete.description: Delete a stack definition if no running instances link to it

post.description: Add a new chart configuration to a stack definition
post.summary: Add a chart to a definition

put.description: Update a chart configuration within a stack definition

delete.description: Remove a chart configuration from a stack definition

get.description: Check if the source template has a newer version than the definition's current version
get.summary: Check if a template upgrade is available

get.description: Export a stack definition and its chart configs as a portable JSON bundle

post.description: Upgrade a definition to the latest template version, adding new charts and updating defaults
post.summary: Apply a template upgrade to a definition

get.description: List stack instances with server-side pagination. Supports page/pageSize or legacy limit/offset params. Use owner=me to filter by the authenticated user. Filter precedence: owner > name > pagination (only the first matching filter is applied).

parameters.description: Filter by owner (use 'me' for current user)
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: Legacy: number of results to skip
parameters.name: offset

200.description: data: []StackInstance, total: int, page: int, pageSize: int

post.description: Clean multiple stack instances in a single request. Uninstalls Helm releases and deletes namespaces.
Delete multiple stack instances in a single request. Processes instances sequentially.
Deploy multiple stack instances in a single request. Processes instances sequentially.
Stop multiple stack instances in a single request. Processes instances sequentially.

get.description: Compare the merged values of two stack instances side-by-side, per chart
Returns the 5 most recently updated stack instances owned by the current user
get.summary: Get recent stack instances for the authenticated user

get.description: Get a stack instance by ID

put.description: Update a stack instance (branch, name, etc.)

delete.description: Deletes a stack instance. If the instance has running resources (status running/stopped/error), a cleanup is initiated first — helm releases are uninstalled and the namespace is deleted before the database record is removed. Returns 204 for immediate deletion (draft instances) or 202 when async cleanup is required.

202.description: Cleanup initiated, instance will be deleted after resources are removed

204.description: No Content — instance deleted immediately (no resources to clean)

409.description: Instance is in a transient state (deploying/stopping/cleaning)

post.description: Dispatches to the action subscriber webhook and wraps its response in an envelope containing action, instance_id, status_code, and result fields. The subscriber's JSON body is nested under the result key. Returns 200 even for non-2xx subscriber responses — check status_code to distinguish.
post.summary: Invoke a registered action against a stack instance

parameters.description: Optional parameters passed through to the subscriber
parameters.name: request

404.description: Instance not found or action not registered

get.description: List all per-chart branch overrides for a stack instance
get.summary: List branch overrides for an instance

put.description: Upsert a per-chart branch override for a specific chart in a stack instance
put.summary: Set or update branch override for a chart

delete.description: Remove the per-chart branch override for a specific chart in a stack instance
delete.summary: Delete branch override for a chart

post.description: Uninstall all Helm releases and delete the K8s namespace, returning the instance to draft status
Create a new stack instance as a copy of an existing one
Trigger Helm deployment for a stack instance

get.description: Get deployment log history for a stack instance. Supports cursor-based pagination for efficient large dataset traversal.

parameters.description: Cursor from previous page for cursor-based pagination (overrides offset)
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns the merged Helm values that were used for a specific deployment
get.summary: Get values snapshot for a deployment log entry

get.description: Compare pending merged values against last-deployed values per chart

post.description: Extend the expiry time of a stack instance. Uses provided ttl_minutes or the instance's existing TTLMinutes.

get.description: List all value overrides for a stack instance

put.description: Upsert value overrides for a specific chart in a stack instance
put.summary: Set or update override for a chart

get.description: Returns detailed pod health including container states, conditions, and recent events
Retrieve the per-instance resource quota override for a stack instance
get.summary: Get quota override for an instance

put.description: Upsert the per-instance resource quota override for a stack instance
put.summary: Set or update quota override for an instance

delete.description: Remove the per-instance resource quota override for a stack instance
delete.summary: Delete quota override for an instance

post.description: Rollback all Helm releases in a stack instance to their previous revision

get.description: Get detailed Kubernetes resource status for a stack instance

post.description: Trigger Helm uninstall for a stack instance

get.description: Generate and export merged values for all charts as a zip archive
Generate and export merged values.yaml for a specific chart
List published templates for regular users, all templates for devops/admin. Includes definition_count and owner_username. Supports server-side pagination.

200.description: Paginated list with data, total, page, pageSize

post.description: Create a new stack template (devops/admin only)
Delete multiple stack templates in a single request. Only unpublished templates with no linked definitions can be deleted.
Publish multiple stack templates in a single request, making them visible to all users.
Unpublish multiple stack templates in a single request, hiding them from regular users.

get.description: Get a stack template by ID, including its chart configurations

put.description: Update an existing stack template (devops/admin only)

delete.description: Delete a stack template if no definitions link to it (devops/admin only)

post.description: Add a new chart configuration to a stack template
post.summary: Add a chart to a template

put.description: Update a chart configuration within a stack template

delete.description: Remove a chart configuration from a stack template

post.description: Create a new draft template that is a copy of the source (devops/admin only)
Create a StackDefinition and ChartConfigs from a template
Make a template visible to all users (devops/admin only)
Instantiate a template, create an instance, set branch overrides, and trigger deployment in a single call
Hide a template from regular users (devops/admin only)

get.description: List all version snapshots for a template, ordered newest first
Compare two template version snapshots side by side
Get a specific template version with its parsed snapshot
Returns all registered users. Admin only. PasswordHash is never included.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a user account. Admin only. Cannot delete own account.

get.description: Returns all API keys for the given user. Admin or own user only.
get.summary: List API keys for a user

post.description: Generates a new API key. An expiration is required: set expires_at (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339) or expires_in_days (positive int), but not both. If API_KEY_MAX_LIFETIME_DAYS is configured, the expiry must not exceed the limit. The raw key is returned once in raw_key and cannot be retrieved again.
post.summary: Create an API key for a user

delete.description: Deletes the specified API key. Admin or own user only.

put.description: Disables a user account. All API keys for this user immediately stop working. Admin only.
Re-enables a previously disabled user account. Admin only.
Resets the password for a local/service account user. Admin only.

get.description: Get API health status
Get API liveness status
Get API readiness status
Upgrades the HTTP connection to a WebSocket for real-time events. Requires a valid JWT token via ?token= query parameter or Authorization: Bearer header.

prefix.description: first 16 chars of raw key for display

build_pipeline_id.description: CI pipeline ID to trigger for image builds

dry_run.description: If true, only report matches without acting

registry_url.description:
Registry fields for automatic image pull secret provisioning.
When RegistryURL is non-empty, a docker-registry secret is created/refreshed
in each stack namespace before chart installs.

version.description: For optimistic locking (1 = initial; 0 = not provided)

build_pipeline_id.description: CI pipeline ID to trigger for image builds
